raw_text:
Confirmed — assignments are correct and the execution order is good.

Only tweak: run bd-1mx in parallel, merge last

Let MaroonHarbor work bd-1mx immediately (it’s isolated), but merge it after bd-3v3 so you don’t accidentally “paper over” an outbox correctness issue with looser concurrency assertions.

So the practical merge order:

bd-2uw (audit deadlock gate)

bd-2qi (mutation_class casts)

bd-13k (TIMESTAMP mismatch)

bd-3v3 (outbox atomicity — correctness-critical)

bd-1mx (concurrency assertions — merge last)

Stop-the-line reminders

bd-2uw: prefer pg_advisory_lock around migration execution (works even if tests/CI become multi-process later). Process-local Once is acceptable only if you can guarantee single process.

bd-3v3: do not “fix” by weakening the atomicity invariant. If outbox is missing, either migrations aren’t applied or the code path truly isn’t enqueuing inside the tx — fix the real cause.

bd-1mx: assertions must still prove uniqueness + legal transitions + final state; only drop brittle exact-count expectations.

Proceed.
